To: T. Adeyemi
From: PC
Subject: WBS v0.1 — review

T,

I have gone through the draft and have some specific structural improvements to propose. I think with the changes below the WBS will be ready to baseline by end of week.

1. Numbering and formatting. I would recommend adopting a four-digit numbering scheme (1.0.0.0) for consistency, and adding a "Status" column to each leaf so we can track progress at the WBS level. I can produce a cleaner version in Excel with these conventions.

2. Decomposition of build packages. 1.2.1 (Teller application) has good depth — seven leaves at level 4. To match that depth I would propose decomposing 1.2.2 (Core banking integration) into the following: 1.2.2.1 API design, 1.2.2.2 API build, 1.2.2.3 API test, 1.2.2.4 Connectivity, 1.2.2.5 Data mapping, 1.2.2.6 Error handling, 1.2.2.7 Monitoring. Similar approach for 1.2.3 (Customer records migration).

3. Testing branch. I would suggest expanding 1.3 to also include 1.3.4 Performance testing, 1.3.5 Security testing, 1.3.6 Accessibility testing, 1.3.7 Regression testing. This gives us a more comprehensive test plan and aligns with industry standards.

4. Rollout waves. The wave numbering is good. I would propose adding a "Go/no-go decision" line after each wave (1.4.1.1, 1.4.2.1, etc.) so the gating decisions are visible in the WBS.

5. Visual presentation. Once we have the cleaned-up version, I can produce a one-page WBS diagram (top three levels) for the COO that summarises the project shape. This will help with the budget conversation by giving her a visual to anchor the numbers to.

I think these changes give us a more polished, more complete-looking artefact. We can baseline by Thursday and forward to the workstream leads on Friday for their information.

Let me know if you want me to proceed with the Excel rebuild and the COO diagram.

PC
